title: Error handling removed in BssaUnBlind
problem: The function ignores the return value of BN_Bn2Bn. If the function fails (e.g., memory allocation in BnExtend), z is zeroized and the computation proceeds with z=0, causing incorrect cryptographic output instead of returning an error.
code:
(void)BN_Bin2Bn(z, input, inputLen);
GOTO_ERR_IF(BN_ModMul(s, z, blind->rInv, n, opt), ret);
fix:
GOTO_ERR_IF(BN_Bin2Bn(z, input, inputLen), ret);
GOTO_ERR_IF(BN_ModMul(s, z, blind->rInv, n, opt), ret);

title: NULL check removed in X509_AddCAPath
problem: The NULL check for val parameter was removed. If val is NULL but valLen is a valid number, the function will crash at line 419 when calling memcmp(existPath, caPath, valLen) where caPath is NULL.

title: Error handling removed for BN_SubLimb
problem: BN_SubLimb can fail with CRYPT_NULL_INPUT or CRYPT_MEM_ALLOC_FAIL. Ignoring the error means if nMinusOne allocation fails, the comparison with n-1 is skipped, potentially accepting invalid input values.

title: NULL check removed in TcpGetFd
problem: The function no longer checks if uio->ctx is NULL before accessing ctx->fd. While uio->ctx should be non-NULL after BSL_UIO_New, this reduces robustness.

title: NULL check removed in TcpSetFd
problem: The function no longer checks if uio->ctx is NULL before accessing ctx->fd. TcpSocketCtrl checks uio->ctx == NULL but TcpSetFd can be called directly.

title: Unnecessary memory allocation in RSA_GetPublicExp
problem: r1 and r2 are allocated with bits size instead of bits>>1. Since p-1 and q-1 (where p and q are roughly bits/2 sized primes) fit in bits/2 + 1 bits, using bits size is wasteful for a bin size optimization PR.

title: Redundant NULL check removed in DRBG_CtrXor
problem: The NULL check for dst was removed. While all callers pass stack-allocated temp buffer, this reduces defensive programming.

title: memcpy_s error handling removed
problem: Ignoring memcpy_s return value removes bounds checking validation, though the code ensures valid sizes.
